items.definitionEn: To go up to a higher level or amount.
items.exampleEn: The population rose from 2 million in 2000 to 3 million in 2020.

items.definitionEn: To go up gradually in number or level.
items.exampleEn: Sales climbed steadily from 200 units in January to 450 in June.

items.definitionEn: To increase suddenly and powerfully in a short time.
items.exampleEn: Internet usage surged by 40% between 2005 and 2010.

items.definitionEn: To rise very quickly to a high level.
items.exampleEn: House prices soared by over 80% during the decade.

items.definitionEn: To increase very rapidly and suddenly.
items.exampleEn: The price of oil rocketed from $40 to $110 per barrel.

items.definitionEn: To rise suddenly by a large amount.
items.exampleEn: Unemployment jumped from 5% to 9% in just two years.

items.definitionEn: To become twice as much or as many.
items.exampleEn: The number of cars doubled from 1 million in 1990 to 2 million in 2010.

items.definitionEn: To go down to a lower level or amount.
items.exampleEn: Production fell from 500 tonnes in 2018 to 320 tonnes in 2022.

items.definitionEn: To decrease in number, amount or quality.
items.exampleEn: Coal consumption declined steadily from 40% to 22% over twenty years.

items.definitionEn: To fall very quickly and by a large amount.
items.exampleEn: Stock prices plummeted by 35% in the final quarter of 2008.

items.definitionEn: To fall to a lower level or amount.
items.exampleEn: The unemployment rate dropped from 8% to 5% between 2015 and 2019.

items.definitionEn: To suddenly decrease by a very large amount.
items.exampleEn: Tourist arrivals plunged from 12 million to just 3 million in 2020.

items.definitionEn: To decrease slightly, often temporarily.
items.exampleEn: Sales dipped briefly in March before recovering to 1,200 units.

items.definitionEn: To reduce by half, or to become half as much.
items.exampleEn: The figure halved from 800,000 in 2010 to 400,000 in 2020.

items.definitionEn: To stay at the same level without changing.
items.exampleEn: The figure remained stable at around 50% throughout the period.

items.definitionEn: To stop rising or falling and become steady.
items.exampleEn: After rising sharply, the numbers levelled off at 25,000 in 2015.

items.definitionEn: To reach a level and then stay constant.
items.definitionRu: Достигать плато, оставаться на одном уровне.
items.exampleEn: Production plateaued at 4,000 units per month after 2018.

items.definitionEn: To remain at the same level without significant change.
items.exampleEn: Interest rates held steady at 2% for the entire decade.

items.definitionEn: To remain unchanged over a period of time.
items.exampleEn: The price of bread stayed constant at $2 between 2010 and 2014.

items.definitionEn: To stay near a particular level with only minor changes.
items.exampleEn: Unemployment hovered around 6% for most of the 1990s.

items.definitionEn: To continue at exactly the same level.
items.exampleEn: Energy consumption remained unchanged at 120 TWh from 2015 to 2018.

items.definitionEn: To change frequently in level or amount, going up and down.
items.exampleEn: Sales fluctuated between 800 and 1,200 units throughout the year.

items.definitionEn: To move repeatedly between two levels or values.
items.exampleEn: Temperatures oscillated between 15 and 25 degrees during the season.

items.definitionEn: To be different at different times or in different situations.
items.exampleEn: Rainfall varied considerably, ranging from 50mm to 200mm per month.

items.definitionEn: To move unsteadily up and down or back and forth.
items.exampleEn: The figure wavered around 40% before settling at 38% in 2020.

items.definitionEn: To change from one level to another and back, often noticeably.
items.exampleEn: Profits swung from a $5 million loss to a $7 million gain within two years.

items.definitionEn: To change very irregularly and unpredictably.
items.exampleEn: Oil prices fluctuated wildly between $30 and $140 per barrel.

items.definitionEn: To rise and fall repeatedly over a period.
items.exampleEn: Visitor numbers went up and down throughout the decade, ranging from 1m to 3m.

items.definitionEn: Suddenly and by a large amount.
items.exampleEn: The number of subscribers increased sharply from 100,000 to 500,000.

items.definitionEn: In a sudden, extreme and very noticeable way.
items.exampleEn: Car ownership grew dramatically, tripling between 1980 and 2010.

items.definitionEn: Slowly and steadily over a period of time.
items.exampleEn: Literacy rates gradually improved from 70% to 92% over fifty years.

items.definitionEn: By a very small amount or to a small degree.
items.exampleEn: The figure rose slightly, from 22% to 24%, during the same period.

items.definitionEn: In a large and noticeable way.
items.exampleEn: Energy use fell significantly, by more than 30%, between 2000 and 2020.

items.definitionEn: By a very small amount that is barely noticeable.
items.exampleEn: Sales increased marginally, by just 1%, from 2018 to 2019.

items.definitionEn: At a regular and constant rate.
items.exampleEn: The population grew steadily by about 2% each year.

items.definitionEn: By a large amount; to a great extent.
items.exampleEn: Spending on healthcare rose considerably, from $200bn to $350bn.

items.definitionEn: Greater in number or amount than something else.
items.exampleEn: More than 60% of respondents preferred online shopping over in-store purchases.

items.definitionEn: Smaller in number or amount than something else.
items.exampleEn: Less than 15% of energy came from renewable sources in 2010.

items.definitionEn: Two times the number of something.
items.exampleEn: There were twice as many female graduates as male ones in 2020.

items.definitionEn: Equal in amount, level or value to something else.
items.exampleEn: The figure for France was the same as that for Germany at 18%.

items.definitionEn: Used to show that a number or amount is not exact.
items.exampleEn: Approximately 40% of the population lived in urban areas in 1950.

items.definitionEn: Approximately; not exactly.
items.exampleEn: Roughly one in three households owned a computer by the year 2000.

items.definitionEn: Three times the level or amount of something else.
items.exampleEn: CO2 emissions in China were three times higher than those in the UK by 2015.

items.definitionEn: Used to show similarities or differences between two things.
items.exampleEn: Sales rose by 25% compared with the previous year's figures.

items.definitionEn: To reach the highest point or level.
items.exampleEn: Visitor numbers peaked at 4.5 million in 2019 before declining sharply.

items.definitionEn: To attain the maximum level of a specific value.
items.exampleEn: Temperatures reached a high of 42 degrees in July 2003.

items.definitionEn: To fall to the lowest level of a specific value.
items.exampleEn: Sales hit a low of just 200 units in February before recovering.

items.definitionEn: To reach the lowest point before starting to rise again.
items.exampleEn: The market bottomed out at 1,200 points in March before recovering.

items.definitionEn: The minimum value or level in a set of data.
items.exampleEn: Norway recorded the lowest figure at just 3% of total emissions.

items.definitionEn: The maximum value or level in a set of data.
items.exampleEn: China had the highest level of car production at 28 million vehicles.

items.definitionEn: To attain the maximum level before declining.
items.exampleEn: Oil prices reached a peak of $147 per barrel in mid-2008.

items.definitionEn: To fall to the minimum level of a specific value.
items.exampleEn: Unemployment reached a low of 3.5% in late 2019.

items.definitionEn: To form a particular amount or part of something.
items.exampleEn: Coal accounted for 45% of electricity generation in 2005.

items.definitionEn: To form or constitute a part of a whole.
items.exampleEn: Women made up 38% of the workforce in 1990.

items.definitionEn: To consist of or be made up of particular parts.
items.exampleEn: The sector comprised approximately 20% of total exports in 2018.

items.definitionEn: To form or make up a particular share of something.
items.exampleEn: Renewable energy constituted 12% of the total energy mix in 2015.

items.definitionEn: To form a particular share or proportion of a whole.
items.exampleEn: Asian students represented nearly 35% of the international enrolment.

items.definitionEn: To occupy a particular proportion of a whole.
items.exampleEn: Housing costs took up roughly 30% of household expenditure in 2020.

items.definitionEn: The part or share of a total that something represents.
items.exampleEn: The proportion of people working from home rose from 5% to 25% by 2021.

items.definitionEn: The part of a total amount that belongs to one category.
items.exampleEn: The share of electric vehicles in new sales reached 18% in 2022.
